patch.value: Bare-core audit extraction phase 2: move audit command implementations and audit flags out of default CLI/SDK into pm-governance-audit

User directive 2026-07-12: default pm CLI and public SDK must not include ANY audit commands or flags; all audit surface lives in the first-party audit plugin. EXTEND packages/pm-governance-audit (packages-first rule) — do NOT create a parallel pm-audit package; optionally add an 'audit' package alias. Phase 1 (closed pm-ixt3) moved registration only — implementations, SDK exports, and all audit flags are still core. Full inventory, scope fence, mechanisms, decisions, and slice order in body.

src/cli/commands/{dedupe-audit,dedupe-merge,comments-audit,normalize}.ts no longer exist in core src; implementations live in packages/pm-governance-audit; runDedupeAudit/runCommentsAudit/runDedupeMerge/runNormalize removed from public SDK exports (sdk/index.ts, sdk/runtime.ts); bare-core pm --help, completion, contracts, and MCP tool definitions contain no --audit or --allow-audit-* surface; installed pm-governance-audit restores every removed command and flag with identical behavior; coverage gate, contracts:update, tool-count assertions, docs, and token-surface baseline all reconciled

# Bare-core audit extraction — phase 2 program plan (filed 2026-07-12, directed pass)

## Goal (user directive)
Default `pm` CLI and public SDK must not include ANY audit commands or audit flags. All audit surface lives in the separate first-party audit plugin. Target package = existing `packages/pm-governance-audit` (packages-first: EXTEND, never create a parallel pm-audit package). Optionally add an `audit` alias to its `pm.aliases` so `pm install audit` works — record under D1.

## Current state (verified 2026-07-12 on main @ d9507407)
Phase 1 (closed pm-ixt3, parent pm-59gj) extracted command REGISTRATION only: bare core no longer registers `dedupe-audit` / `dedupe-merge` / `comments-audit` / `normalize`; the package registers them but its runtime (packages/pm-governance-audit/extensions/governance-audit/runtime.ts, `loadGovernanceModule` via PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT) dynamically imports the implementations FROM core SDK at run time.

Still shipping in default core:
1. **Implementations (~2,146 LOC)**: src/cli/commands/dedupe-audit.ts (639), dedupe-merge.ts (486), comments-audit.ts (460), normalize.ts (561) — bundled with every CLI install.
2. **Public SDK exports**: src/sdk/index.ts:276–281 and src/sdk/runtime.ts:429–453 export runDedupeAudit / runCommentsAudit / runDedupeMerge / runNormalize.
3. **Core-registered CLI flags**:
   - `--audit` on `pm files` and `pm docs` (src/cli/register-mutation.ts ~3163 and ~3221) — cross-item linked-path usage report mode handled inside runFilesAction / runDocsAction.
   - `--allow-audit-update`, `--allow-audit-dep-update` on `pm update` / `update-many` (src/cli/registration-helpers.ts:527–532 anyOptionTrue merge; src/cli/commands/update-many.ts:59–60; normalize passthrough src/cli/commands/normalize.ts:96/494/501).
   - `--allow-audit-comment` (src/cli/commands/comments.ts:41), `--allow-audit-note` + `--allow-audit-comment` (notes), `--allow-audit-learning` + `--allow-audit-comment` (src/cli/commands/learnings.ts:33–35) — merged into `allowAuditBypass` (register-mutation.ts:1376/1671/1714).
   - `--allow-audit-release` (src/cli/register-operations.ts:573).
4. **SDK contracts**: src/sdk/cli-contracts/flag-contracts.ts — dedupe/comments audit flag tables (~256/~286), `--audit` entries (~352/~368), allow-audit-* entries (~314–336, ~809, ~1203–1348); tool-option-contracts.ts:285–286 (camelCase MCP param mappings); tool-parameter-tables.ts; tool-schema.ts.
5. **MCP**: src/mcp/tool-definitions.ts:392 — pm_files description advertises the audit mode; allowAudit* params flow through the shared option contracts; pm_run doc line ~58 already frames dedupe-audit/comments-audit/normalize as dynamic package-owned actions (that is the correct end state — keep).
6. **Presentation/UX**: help-content.ts, error-guidance.ts:703 (ownership-conflict guidance names the audit flags), core/diagnostics/remediation.ts, shell completion (bash+zsh+fish), docs/ (12 files mention audit), core/profile/profile-presets.ts, core/telemetry/observability.ts.

## Scope fence
- **IN**: the four command implementations + their SDK exports; the `--audit` files/docs report mode; the six `--allow-audit-*` flags; every contract/MCP/completion/help/docs entry for those.
- **OUT (stays core)**: the audit-protection ENFORCEMENT itself (ownership/audit guard in core store + governance issue codes + the error-guidance path for ownership conflicts); history-redact / history-compact / history-repair ("audited history stream" is a different sense of the word); `pm validate` checks; the package's existing onRead/onWrite hook log.

## Mechanisms (verified in source)
- `ExtensionApi.registerFlags(targetCommand, FlagDefinition[])` (core/extensions/extension-types.ts:1363) — plugin-owned flags on existing core commands; parsed via the extensionFlagDefinitions plumbing in cli/main.ts (~:948–:1133). Core option reads are generic (anyOptionTrue with alias lists), so core enforcement can keep honoring allowAudit* option VALUES while the plugin owns registration/help/completion.
- `ExtensionApi.registerCommand(command, override)` — plugin may override `pm files` / `pm docs` to add the --audit report mode, or ship the report as a package-owned command.
- Package code is TypeScript-only (.ts entries, native type-stripping loader — pm-2p7a); installed npm extensions untracked in git (pm-sod3).

## Decisions needed BEFORE S2/S3 (see Decision child)
- **D1**: handling mechanism for plugin-owned flags on core commands — registerFlags + registerCommand override vs package-owned subcommands; plus whether to add the `audit` package alias.
- **D2**: bare-core behavior when the plugin is absent — do non-owner audit-gated mutations fall back to `--force` only, or hard-block? Does the SDK keep a *programmatic* allowAuditBypass option (not a CLI flag), and what is the breaking-change posture for deleting the four SDK exports (older published package versions dynamic-import them; first-party is covered by same-cut date-based lockstep + pm_min_version bump, but external SDK consumers lose the exports)?

## Sequencing & risks
- pm-qfdd (commander program singleton leaks dynamic extension flags across in-process invocations) — affects in-process tests of plugin-registered flags; prefer worker-bridge suites (pm-kvd0 pattern); coordinate if qfdd still open at implementation time.
- pm-kb5h / pm-yh8r fences: files move WITH their owning slice — these four files leave src/ under THIS program; cite pm-vjk3, not kb5h.
- Coverage: 100% literal all-src gate loses ~2,146 LOC from src/; the package needs equivalent spec coverage; vitest.config + `pnpm contracts:update` + the tool-count assertion in 4 places all need reconciliation.
- Token-surface win expected (help + MCP tools/list shrink): re-measure with scripts/measure-agent-token-surface.mjs and record on the pm-mpbb ledger.
- Release coordination: package pm_min_version bump in the same daily cut (date-based lockstep, scripts/sync-versions.mjs).

## Slice order (children)
S1 relocate implementations + drop SDK exports (no decision dependency — start here) → D1/D2 decision → S2 `--audit` files/docs mode → S3 `--allow-audit-*` family → S4 contracts/MCP/help/docs purge + token re-measure → S5 coverage + bare-vs-installed e2e verification.

## Entry point for next session
Read this body + children top-down. D1/D2 are maintainer decisions (decision_needed queue). S1 is actionable immediately.
